detection_signals: Warm vintages are always better than cool vintages
The hottest vintage always produces the best wine
Every region makes better wine in warm years
why_incorrect: While adequate warmth is necessary for ripening, the relationship is not linear. Warmer vintages can produce ripe grapes, but may result in lower acidity. Excessive heat can stress vines.
misconception: Warmer vintages always produce riper, higher-quality wines because grapes ripen more fully.
corrected_understanding: Warmer vintages can produce riper fruit, but quality depends on balance. Excessive heat can create unbalanced wines. Quality results from good match between vintage conditions and terroir.
